key_insight: Orbital friction welding keeps relative motion uniform across the interface, enabling sound joints and non-axisymmetric geometry that rotary friction welding cannot easily accommodate.
engineering_relevance: The study shows a practical route to expand solid-state joining from simple round parts to more complex component shapes with controlled heat input and lower distortion.
method: Built one custom test rig capable of both orbital friction welding (OFW) and rotary friction welding (RFW)
Joined S235JRC+C steel under directly comparable process conditions
Evaluated weld quality with tensile tests, metallographic cross-sections, and hardness measurements
evidence_signal: Comparative testing showed OFW produced metallurgically sound joints with promising mechanical performance, while the new high-speed rig achieved higher relative velocities than prior work.
watch_item: Performance gains depend on maintaining tight control of kinematics and heat generation, especially when scaling to different geometries or materials.
paper_title: Extending geometric flexibility in friction welding: A comparative study of orbital and rotary friction welding
